name: codex-apply-patch-solid-deny
doc: THE load-bearing Codex parity scenario. apply_patch is Codex's PRIMARY edit primitive: `tool_name:"apply_patch"`, `tool_input:{command:<freeform patch>}` — no file_path/content, so before the parser landed the SOLID/DRY/protected-path gates saw `filePath: undefined` and enforcement was 0% on the platform's main write path (enforcement theatre). normalize.ts now fans the patch into `event.files` (adapters/codex/apply-patch.ts) and handle-pre.ts:70 runs applyPatchGate over each file. Step 1: a 150-line `*** Add File` (judged like a Write) → SOLID file-size deny on the codex envelope. Step 2: a small add → allowed (empty): the apply_patch gate runs only the pure file gates, not the stateful APEX freshness gate, so a compliant hunk is silent. Real output verified 2026-07-06 against `bun src/cli/bin.ts hook codex core`.

steps.doc: 150-line Add File > 100 → parseApplyPatch reconstructs the new file from the `+` lines, applyPatchGate maps `add`→Write and evaluate() denies on file-size. Assert the [BLOCKED] SOLID tag survives into the codex permissionDecisionReason. This is the exact hole the 2026-07-06 audit flagged, now closed and mechanically locked.

A small compliant Add File → allowed → empty stdout. The apply_patch gate runs only the pure file gates (protected-path, file-size, DRY), never the stateful APEX freshness gate (its filePath was always undefined, so it never policed apply_patch), so a compliant hunk is silent — unlike a small single-file Write, which the APEX gate blocks on a fresh session.

Multi-file envelope: a compliant `ok.ts` (1 line) AND an oversized `big.ts` (150 lines) in ONE patch → DENY. applyPatchGate ORs the per-file verdicts (handle-pre.ts:71) — one violating hunk blocks the WHOLE patch, so Codex cannot smuggle an oversized file past the gate by bundling it with a clean one. The deny names `big.ts` (the offending file). Verified 2026-07-06.

A Delete File hunk on its own → allowed (empty stdout). applyPatchGate skips file-size/DRY for `delete` (no content), running only the protected-path check; an unprotected delete is silent. Proves the gate does not blanket-deny apply_patch, only violating hunks. Verified 2026-07-06.

tool_input.command:
*** Begin Patch
*** Delete File: obsolete.ts
*** End Patch
